# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd default authorization policy for a namespace. Configure policy to handle services without explicit authorization rules. Set up default behavior for service-to-service communication. This is for our service mesh policy framework.

Write secure code.
# Category: linkerd_policy_default_allow

apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: ServiceMeshPolicy
metadata:
  name: default
spec:
  selector: {} # Select all services in the namespace by selecting none (i.e., no label) for this policy to apply on them, and then define rules later using 'rules' field below
---  
apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: ServiceMeshPolicyRule
metadata:
  name: default-authorization
spec:
  action: allow # This rule will permit all traffic to the service (i.e., no explicit authorizations) by setting 'action' field below as "allow" in this case, and then define rules later using 'rules' or 'matchingRules' fields above for more specific control over accessibility
---  
apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: ServiceMeshPolicyRule
metadata:
  name: default-serviceToServiceCommunicationBehavior
spec:
  action: allow # This rule will permit all traffic to the service (i.e., no explicit authorizations) by setting 'action' field below as "allow" in this case, and then define rules later using 'rules' or 'matchingRules' fields above for more specific control over accessibility
